---
title: "The AI peer review"
description: "Two isolated referees, a 19-point technical audit with blocking authority, and synthesis quality control."
---

The referees review **RECAST's own extension** — the generated report and the
executed code — not the original paper, which was already refereed by its journal.
The doctrine is Berk, Harvey & Hirshleifer (2017): judge importance first;
separate *essential* issues (the result can't be trusted without the fix, argued
scientifically) from *suggestions* (non-binding); no hunches, no
robustness-for-its-own-sake.

## Two isolated referees

Each referee is a separate agent with its own system prompt and access only to the
files it is allowed to read — neither sees the other's report.

- **General referee** — identification inheritance, replication fidelity, the
  suspect-controls flag, and whether the claims are proportionate to the evidence.
  Routes severity through a standard tree (does it invalidate the claim? is it
  fixable?). Cannot block.
- **DML-technical referee** — a strict canonical audit (**C1–C19** plus absorbed
  general-DML checks) of the *executed* code against the Baiardi–Naghi /
  Chernozhukov standards: interaction bases, repetition count, SE-aggregation
  formula, generic-ML proxy construction, model-class-vs-treatment routing,
  clustering, functional-form wording, and more. It is the **only** referee with
  authority to mark an issue **blocking** — which forces a code re-run — and only
  when a canonical fix would plausibly change sign, significance, or magnitude by
  more than 20%.

## Synthesis and the round budget

A **synthesis referee** is the first agent to see both reports. Its job is quality
control, not aggregation: it deduplicates, downgrades any "essential" raised
without a scientific argument, enforces proportionality (0–3 essential issues is
typical), and respects the technical referee's exclusive blocking authority. A
**revision agent** — the only writer of the report and notebooks — applies the
fixes. The default budget is **two rounds**, with a third only if essential issues
remain.

## It catches real bugs

This is not ceremony. On the teacher-training paper, the technical referee issued a
genuine **blocking** finding: the randomization strata declared in the spec were
never carried into the covariate sets — a control-discipline violation on the
headline heterogeneity estimate. It was fixed in the shared library and the stage
re-ran. (Notably, restoring the strata moved the estimate only slightly, which
*eliminated* that explanation for the benchmark divergence rather than closing it —
an honest outcome, reported as such.) Across the test battery, the review loop and
the build surfaced and fixed several framework bugs that the first paper could not
have exposed.
